methods.description: A method for simulating climate forcing in ice-sheet models using a matrix of pre-calculated GCM simulation outputs, allowing interpolation between different climate states based on model state parameters.

steps.description: Using the definition by Pollard (2010), a climate matrix is a collection of pre-calculated output data from several steady-state GCM simulations, called 'snapshots', that differ from each other in one or more key parameters.

When performing a simulation with an ice-sheet model, at every point in time during the simulation the prescribed climate forcing is determined by combining the climate states constituting the matrix according to the position of the model state within the matrix.
steps.searchable_summary: combine climate states based on model state

steps.description: When the ice-sheet model is in a state corresponding to one of the GCM snapshots, the climate from this snapshot will be prescribed.
steps.searchable_summary: prescribe climate from matching GCM snapshot

steps.description: When the ice-sheet model is in a state lying in between different GCM snapshots, the prescribed climate is an interpolation of these snapshots.

methods.description: An inverse forward modeling approach to determine pCO2 based on the difference between modelled and observed benthic d 18 O, adapted to use the climate matrix for regional climate determination.

steps.description: The modelled benthic d 18 O is calculated using ice volume, ice-sheet d 18 O and deep-water temperatures relative to PD for every 100 years.
Adapt the approach for our model set-up by using the difference between modelled and observed d 18 O to calculate a value for pCO2, which is subsequently forwarded to the climate matrix.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate pCO2 from d 18 O difference

steps.description: The scaling factor changes to 120 ppmv / per mill d 18 O change in order to produce a glacial interglacial contrast of 90 ppmv pCO2.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust scaling factor for pCO2 calculation

steps.description: For forcing the inverse routine, the LR04 stack was used.
steps.searchable_summary: use LR04 stack for inverse routine
